design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed = hash('Super vs Orchids|competitor_comparison|people comparing Super with Orchids|2026-07-06T03:53:23.749171+00:00') → hero=comparison-lab, typography=Cabinet Grotesk, components=[decision-matrix, workflow-playbook-timeline, source-card-dossier], gsap=[card-stacking, hover-physics]. AIDA Check: Attention hero → Interest comparison + proof → Desire workflow + media → Action CTA band. Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk from Fontshare. Hero Math Verification: max-width 1120px, clamp sizing yields 2–3 lines. Bento Density Verification: compare-grid uses grid-auto-flow:dense. GSAP Motion Selection: card-stacking on scroll, hover-physics on cards. Label Sweep & Button Check: black pill primary CTA, white secondary CTA, all buttons legible on paper background.
headline: Super vs Orchids: choosing a personal AI agent for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Orchids. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats.
site_brief.title: Super vs Orchids

summary: A fair, operator-focused comparison of Super and Orchids for people evaluating personal AI agents. Orchids represents experimental automation approaches in the broader agent market. Super is positioned as the sharper alternative for repeated computer-use workflows because it can operate real interfaces and reuse a computer-use cache over time.
